text: Writing clean code is essential for creating software that is easy to understand and modify. The most important goal is to make your code readable, as if it were written for another person rather than for a computer. Start by following clear naming conventions; use descriptive names for variables and functions that explain their purpose. When creating functions, keep them short and focused on a single task. This improves maintainability because each part of the code has a clear, simple responsibility. By writing clean code, you save time for yourself and your teammates when fixing bugs or adding new features.
